key_themes: Smooth CEO transition to Greg Abel with early signals of continuity (buybacks, personal stock purchases)
Record cash pile (~$370B+) positioning Berkshire for opportunistic deployments and insurance moves
Defensive safe-haven qualities amid 2026 market volatility
Attractive valuation after YTD pullback and losing streak, viewed as a buying opportunity
Long-term value discipline and resilience across market conditions
summary: Investor sentiment toward BRK.B is positive but measured in mid-April 2026 following Warren Buffett’s handover to Greg Abel on January 1. Abel has quickly signaled continuity by restarting share buybacks and committing his entire 2026 salary to buying Berkshire stock, while the company’s fortress-like cash position (~$370B) is seen as dry powder for deals or insurance growth. Although shares experienced an eight-day losing streak in March, lagged the S&P 500 slightly, and traded around $475 after pulling back from February levels, many investors view the current price as a compelling entry for a proven defensive compounder with strong long-term capital allocation credentials.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/berkshire-hathaway-brk-b-best-143419941.html)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/28/berkshire-shares-suffer-longest-losing-streak-in-more-than-7-years.html)[[3]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/03/19/greg-abel-warren-buffetts-berkshire-hathaway-stock/)
sources: 15+ news articles (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, Barron's, WSJ, Motley Fool on earnings, Abel's first 100 days, safe-haven status), Multiple active Reddit threads (r/BerkshireHathaway, r/ValueInvesting) with investor debates on valuation and outlook, 20+ relevant X posts (semantic + keyword searches, Mar–Apr 2026) showing buying interest and analysis
